classification_rationale: Mechanism-level failure classification of three hard slices from Issue #2751 runtime evidence. All hard slices remained horizon_exhausted; no topology alternative produced clearance. Not benchmark or paper-facing evidence.

slice_diagnosis.evidence_summary: All candidates horizon_exhausted at ~5.2-6.1m progress. Progress-gated rows show 0 switches. Baseline also stuck at 5.2m. reuse_penalty reached 6.1m with 2 switches but still horizon_exhausted.
caveats: Progress-gated thresholds (0.05, 0.1, 0.2) all produced identical outcomes; gate is not the limiting factor.
reuse_penalty produced slightly more progress (6.1m vs 5.2m) but still horizon_exhausted, suggesting the geometry bottleneck is the dominant constraint.

slice_diagnosis.evidence_summary: All candidates identical at 1.45m progress, 0 switches, 159 deadlock steps. No topology variant produced different behavior.
caveats: All five candidates (baseline, reuse_penalty, three progress_gated thresholds) produced exactly the same progress and switch count, making this the cleanest no_useful_topology_alternative signal.

slice_diagnosis.evidence_summary: reuse_penalty and all progress_gated thresholds reached 6.39m with 13 switches but still horizon_exhausted. Baseline only 4.87m with 3 switches. Switching occurs but does not produce clearance.
caveats: Cannot distinguish blocked_geometry from switch_too_often without per-step switch_timeline. 13 switches at same final progress could mean oscillation between equally-bad alternatives.
Per-step switch_timeline data exists only in worktree-local topology_hypotheses.json traces, not in durable git evidence.
baseline achieved 4.87m with 3 switches; reuse_penalty and progress_gated achieved 6.39m with 13 switches, suggesting switching helped reach further but still could not clear the intersection.

negative_control.note: Negative control succeeded with zero topology switching across all candidates, confirming the mechanism does not introduce spurious switching in trivial scenarios.

diagnostic_gaps: Per-step switch_timeline unavailable in durable evidence; prevents separating switch_too_often from candidate_route_blocked for t_intersection_transfer.
No mechanism-diagnosis script exists; classification was manual from summary.json and scout analysis.
Progress-gated thresholds produced identical outcomes per slice, so the gate parameter is not the limiting factor for these scenarios.
